items.title: Single-generation-engine claim confirmed
steps: 1. Grep every seo_data write method caller across src/ and scripts/
items.expected: One engine; every generation path identified and covered by T2
items.evidence: Exactly one engine (util.seo-section-runner.ts), consumed only by service.seo-generation.ts's three wrappers and the two backfill scripts. Remaining seo_data writers: the worker (merges wrapper output — covered), the deterministic enrichment service (read-patch-write, no LLM canonical), and human-input PATCH endpoints (page-editor updatePageSeo, user-website updateSeoDataRaw — out of scope by design). Answers the owner's 'do we have multiple generators' question: no.

items.title: Canonical never LLM-authored again
steps: 1. Remove the canonical_url instruction from SeoGeneration.critical.md (explicit 'do NOT generate' note added)
2. New util.canonical-path.ts deriveCanonicalPath (page: real path; post: /{type-slug}/{slug}; null-on-failure, never throws)
3. Inject after generation in generateSeoForSection (critical section), generateAllSeoSections, generateAllWithSharedContext (covers UI single, UI all, worker)
4. seo-generate-missing.ts switched to the wrapper; private override deleted
items.expected: Deterministic canonical on every generation path regardless of model output
items.evidence: Unit: LLM-fabricated 'https://example.com/totally/fabricated/path/' overridden to /articles/cleanings-101 (post) and /wrong-guess overridden to /services/cleaning (page). Live dev controller-path run: services/apicoectomy generated canonical /services/apicoectomy.

items.title: Snapshot column fixed + GEO auto-apply explicit (owner option A)
steps: 1. Migration previous_content jsonb -> text on dev (batch 126)

2. apply_geo_content / applyGeoContent flag threaded through both generate-all wrappers, default true (0.0.143 behavior preserved); SIDE EFFECT JSDoc on both; byte-count logging on applies; skip-log on opt-out
3. Dev round-trip: snapshot a real 5.4KB HTML post, restore from snapshot, clear
items.expected: The previously-always-crashing snapshot works end to end; opt-out verifiably skips; recovery restores byte-identically
items.evidence: Dev round-trip: snapshot write succeeded (was Postgres 22P02 before), previous_content === original (string), restore byte-identical, snapshot cleared. Live dev generation with defaults: content rewritten (recommendationBytes 348 logged), snapshot captured, restored byte-identically afterward. apply_geo_content:false: unit test proves no createPageVersion/updateContentWithSnapshot; skip-log observed in test output.
items.notes: Prod still runs jsonb until dev/dave -> main deploys the migration — auto-apply on prod keeps failing until then (unchanged from today's status quo; nothing regressed).

items.title: Tests
steps: 1. Extend service.seo-generation.test.ts mocks (PageModel.findRawById, PostTypeModel) + 3 new cases
items.evidence: 161/161 across the suite; tsc 0 errors; check:conventions --strict 0 violations.
